Title:how to download Rosetta or Rosetta 2 - Apple Community
Snippet:The original Rosetta was a byte-code translator for powerpc to Intel that was part of Snow Leopard 10.6.*. Rosetta 2 was introduced with the Apple Silicon Macs and is a byte-code translator for some X86_64 Intel applications. When you attempt to run an 64-bit Intel application compiled for macOS for the first time, a dialog will appear offering to install Rosetta 2.
Link:https://discussions.apple.com/thread/255348239

# Command-line interface (CLI) for headless batch mode¶

Command-line arguments offer a variety of options that generally override the corresponding parameters in the preferences.

The CLI option for mzmine offers to run one batch file (-batch). This batch file can be easily generated by another script

or can be a standardized batch file for an analytical method that is applied to multiple datasets.

The most convenient way to apply a batch file to other datasets is to specify the **-input** and **-output**

arguments to process all import files and to redirect all outputs to another directory. See examples below

for how to process all .mzML files in a folder and to redirect outputs to a new directory and base filename.

Running mzmine in CLI mode requires a user. Either login via graphical-user interface (GUI) or via command-line argument **-login** (web browser support required) or via **-login-console**.

Then locate the user file in your system USER directory under /.mzmine/users/. The most recently active user is automatically selected for mzmine processing,

but an .mzuser file can be passed in as command-line argument to **-user**.

For command-line mode, the mzmine\_console.exe is preferred as it will print the logging output to the console.

An easy way to start mzmine with arguments is to create a shortcut to the mzmine.exe or the mzmine\_console.exe, right-click, and define the target with additional arguments. This example runs mzmine in batch mode (headless), imports the specified batch file, overrides the memory management to **none** (which is the default), effectively using memory mapping to store and access spectral, centroid, and feature data from temporary files stored in the defined temp directory. By leaving out the *memory* or *temp* arguments, the values stored in the current *preferences* file will be used, or the default values if no *preferences* file was found.

**Login to a user in command-line mode**

"C:\Program Files\mzmine\mzmine_console.exe" -login

"C:\Program Files\mzmine\mzmine_console.exe" -login-console

**Process all .mzML files in a folder by the provided batch file, redirect output files to results folder and use a base filename, various export modules add a specific suffix**

"C:\Program Files\mzmine\mzmine_console.exe" -user "D:\user\testuser.mzuser" -batch "D:\batch\my_batch_file.mzbatch" -input "D:\Data\*.mzML" -output "D:\Results\basename"

**Defining the temp folder, otherwise uses the one specified in local configuration**

"C:\Program Files\mzmine\mzmine_console.exe" -user "D:\user\testuser.mzuser" -batch "D:\batch\my_batch_file.mzbatch" -temp "D:\tmpmzmine"

**Start mzmine batch with memory mapping (DEFAULT)**

"C:\Program Files\mzmine\mzmine_console.exe" -user "D:\user\testuser.mzuser" -batch "D:\batch\my_batch_file.mzbatch" -memory none -temp "D:\tmpmzmine"

**Start mzmine batch on machines with enough memory (RAM) with -memory all** this will disable memory mapping to temporary files.

"C:\Program Files\mzmine\mzmine_console.exe" -user "D:\user\testuser.mzuser" -batch "D:\batch\my_batch_file.mzbatch" -memory all -temp "D:\tmpmzmine"

If the app was installed to the *Applications* folder, run this command in the terminal to run a batch file:

/Applications/mzmine.app/Contents/MacOS/mzmine -user "path/to/testuser.mzuser" -batch /path/to/batch_file.mzbatch

/Applications/mzmine.a

answers: FEASIBLE - MZmine exists, supports batch/CLI usage, and can be installed on macOS Apple-Silicon (arm64) via Rosetta 2 emulation. Evidence of batch/CLI support is found in [MZmine documentation](https://mzmine.github.io/mzmine_documentation/commandline_tool.html), and Rosetta 2 compatibility is confirmed in [GitHub Issue #1942](https://github.com/mzmine/mzmine/issues/1942).

Here is the detailed summary of my findings on using MZmine via batch/CLI mode:

1. **Command-line Usage**: MZmine supports CLI batch processing using the `-batch` flag to specify the batch file. Other key flags include:
   - `-input`: Specify input files/directories (e.g., `"D:\Data\*.mzML"`).
   - `-output`: Redirect outputs to a specific directory and base filename.
   - `-user`: Specify a user file (`.mzuser`) for authentication.
   - `-login` or `-login-console`: Log in via CLI.
   - `-temp`: Define a temporary folder for memory mapping.
   - `-memory`: Control memory usage (`none` for memory mapping, `all` for full RAM usage).

2. **Concrete Examples**:
   - **Windows**:
     `"C:\Program Files\mzmine\mzmine_console.exe" -user "D:\user\testuser.mzuser" -batch "D:\batch\my_batch_file.mzbatch" -input "D:\Data\*.mzML" -output "D:\Results\basename"`
   - **macOS**:
     `/Applications/mzmine.app/Contents/MacOS/mzmine -user "path/to/testuser.mzuser" -batch /path/to/batch_file.mzbatch`

3. **Sources**:
   - Official MZmine Documentation: [Command line tool (batch mode)](https://mzmine.github.io/mzmine_documentation/commandline_tool.html)
   - GitHub Issues: Issues like [#1827](https://github.com/mzmine/mzmine/issues/1827) and [#1432](https://github.com/mzmine/mzmine3/issues/1432) discuss CLI usage and batch mode setup.

SEARCH_COMPLETE

Here is the detailed summary of my findings: TECHNICAL_ISSUE: The CSV tools are currently unavailable due to an internal error ("local variable "result" referenced before assignment"). Despite this, I have successfully extracted the following CLI commands and descriptions from the provided information: [{'command': '-batch', 'description': 'Specify the batch file.', 'source_url': 'https://mzmine.github.io/mzmine_documentation/commandline_tool.html'}, {'command': '-input', 'description': 'Specify input files/directories (e.g., "D:\\Data\\*.mzML").', 'source_url': 'https://mzmine.github.io/mzmine_documentation/commandline_tool.html'}, {'command': '-output', 'description': 'Redirect outputs to a specific directory and base filename.', 'source_url': 'https://mzmine.github.io/mzmine_documentation/commandline_tool.html'}, {'command': '-user', 'description': 'Specify a user file (.mzuser) for authentication.', 'source_url': 'https://mzmine.github.io/mzmine_documentation/commandline_tool.html'}, {'command': '-login', 'description': 'Log in via CLI.', 'source_url': 'https://mzmine.github.io/mzmine_documentation/commandline_tool.html'}, {'command': '-login-console', 'description': 'Log in via CLI.', 'source_url': 'https://mzmine.github.io/mzmine_documentation/commandline_tool.html'}, {'command': '-temp', 'description': 'Define a temporary folder for memory mapping.', 'source_url': 'https://mzmine.github.io/mzmine_documentation/commandline_tool.html'}, {'command': '-memory', 'description': 'Control memory usage (none for memory mapping, all for full RAM usage).', 'source_url': 'https://mzmine.github.io/mzmine_documentation/commandline_tool.html'}]. The source for all commands is the official MZmine documentation: [Command line tool (batch mode)](https://mzmine.github.io/mzmine_documentation/commandline_tool.html). To resolve the technical issue, the CSV tool implementation needs to be fixed to ensure proper dataset creation and retrieval.
